articles.title: US and China both believe the other is a declining power
articles.summary: China believes the U.S. is a declining power with expansionist ambitions. The U.S. thinks the same of China.

articles.title: State-of-the-art air defense systems have not been able to stop some Iranian missiles
articles.summary: The U.S. and Israel say they've depleted most of Iran's missile arsenal, but its weapons — including controversial cluster munitions — are challenging even the most advanced air-defense systems.

articles.title: Meta and YouTube Found Negligent in Landmark Social Media Addiction Trial
articles.summary: A jury found the companies harmed a young user with design features that were addictive and led to her mental health distress.

articles.title: Sweden's top general says U.S. remains 'really important ally'
articles.summary: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Sweden's chief of defense, Gen. Michael Claesson, about NATO, the wars in Ukraine and Iran, and Europe's relationship with the United States.

articles.title: Trump Says He’s Talking With Iran. Iran Says He’s Not. Here’s Why.
articles.summary: The president has domestic and international political motivations for touting negotiations to end the war. Iran has similar ones to deny discussions.

articles.title: Doctors worry about FDA scrutiny of RSV shots to protect babies
articles.summary: The antibody shots are about 80% effective at preventing babies from ending up in intensive care because of RSV. The drugmakers behind them maintain they're safe.

articles.title: Meta Lays Off 700 Employees, While Rewarding Top Executives
articles.summary: The jobs cuts and a new stock program for executives come as Meta continues to shift its focus to artificial intelligence.

articles.title: Travelers are facing the longest TSA wait times in history
articles.summary: Wait times are exceeding four hours at some major airports, leading TSA officers to call out at rates of 40 to 50%, according to TSA Deputy Administrator Ha Nguyen McNeill.

articles.title: Iran’s Attacks Force U.S. Troops to Work Remotely
articles.summary: Iran has severely damaged several American military bases in the Middle East, officials say.

articles.title: Advice on Navigating T.S.A. Wait Times at the Airport
articles.summary: With lines stretching for hours and passengers missing flights, fliers are desperate to ease the wait at security. There are some steps you can take, but no guarantees.

articles.title: Greetings from Turkey's border with Iran, where Iranians let loose on the dance floor
articles.summary: Iranians escaping hardship and war are shaking it off to Persian, Arabic and Turkish tunes in this disco in eastern Turkey.

articles.title: Jury orders Meta and Google to pay woman $6 million in social media addiction trial
articles.summary: The verdict marks the end of the first-ever jury trial over whether tech giants should be held accountable for social media addiction. It may influence the outcome of 2,000 other pending lawsuits.

articles.title: For the first time in more than 1,400 years, Church of England gets a woman leader
articles.summary: A new archbishop of Canterbury has been installed in a historic ceremony. Sarah Mullally is the 106th person to hold the job, and the first woman.

articles.title: Why Are Delays at Houston’s Bush Airport Worse Than at Others?
articles.summary: Lines were long at airports nationwide Tuesday amid T.S.A. staffing shortages, but passengers at Bush Intercontinental Airport waited more than four hours to pass through checkpoints.

articles.title: Savannah Guthrie's first interview since mother Nancy vanished: 'I imagine her terror'
articles.summary: Nearly two months after Nancy Guthrie disappeared, her daughter Savannah discusses the toll on her family in an emotional interview with her Today show colleague Hoda Kotb.

articles.title: Air Canada C.E.O. Draws Scorn for Delivering Condolences Only in English
articles.summary: The lack of French in Michael Rousseau’s speech about the deadly collision at LaGuardia Airport reignited a debate over linguistic inclusivity in Canada.

articles.title: At the Legacy Museum, facing America's racist past is a path, not a punishment
articles.summary: "There is an America that is more free — where there's more equality, where there is more justice, where there is less bigotry — and I think it's waiting for us," says lawyer Bryan Stevenson.

articles.title: A leadership vacuum adds to strains on the CDC
articles.summary: Low morale, staff turnover and budget issues have sapped the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. The administration is expected to soon name a new director, who will have their hands full.

articles.title: How Bald Reddit Helps People Manage Hair Loss
articles.summary: A Reddit forum has become a haven for those deciding whether it’s time to shave it all.

articles.title: Thousands of U.S. troops deploy to Middle East. And, the latest on DHS funding talks
articles.summary: The U.S. is sending thousands of paratroopers from the 82nd Airborne to the Middle East. And, congressional Republicans present Democrats with a new deal to fund the Department of Homeland Security.

articles.title: Iran rejects Trump's proposal to end the war and lays out 5 conditions
articles.summary: Iran says no to President Trump's ceasefire plan and publicized a counterproposal that includes safeguards against future attacks on Iran and sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Islandwide blackouts bring life in Cuba to a near total halt
articles.summary: NPR's A Martinez asks CNN's Havana Bureau Chief Patrick Oppmann about Cuba's power blackouts, which have brought the country to a near total halt.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
